name: local-business
description: Structural bias for local business pages that need to convert nearby intent into contact, appointment, or visit.
intent: Establish service clarity and local trust signals early, make contact and booking actions persistent, and resolve geographic and scheduling objections before they prevent action.

sectionOrder.reason: Local visitors need immediate confirmation of service type, location, and that the business is open and accepting customers.
Reviews, years in business, certifications, and local affiliations address the trust deficit that comes from local unfamiliarity.
Visitors must understand what services are offered before they can decide to contact or book.
Hours, location, parking, service area, and booking requirements reduce the friction that prevents follow-through.
Local conversion actions should be easy to find from anywhere on the page without full scroll.

density.reason: Local pages must balance enough evidence to build trust with enough clarity that a busy or mobile visitor can act quickly.

ctaCadence.secondaryActionRule: Surface call, email, and direction actions as co-visible but visually subordinate to the primary booking or appointment action.
ctaCadence.reason: Local intent peaks unpredictably; the contact or booking action must be reachable at multiple scroll positions.

overlayUse.reason: Local business overlays should support the booking and contact flows; critical logistics like hours and phone must never require an overlay.

antiPatterns.name: hidden-phone-or-address
antiPatterns.reason: Local visitors in high-intent moments need contact information immediately visible, not buried or behind an overlay.

antiPatterns.name: no-sticky-action-on-mobile
antiPatterns.reason: Mobile local search intent is high; the booking or contact action must persist as the visitor scrolls.

antiPatterns.name: generic-hero-without-location-signal
antiPatterns.reason: A local business hero that omits the service area, neighborhood, or city loses the trust signal that separates it from national competitors.

antiPatterns.name: proof-without-local-specificity
antiPatterns.reason: Generic five-star testimonials do not build local trust; reviews should reference location, staff, or local context.
